claims.claim: Israel's standard VAT rate is 18% for 2026 (the proposed January 2026 hike to 19% was dropped).
claims.evidence: Knesset raised VAT from 17% to 18% effective 2025-01-01. The Ministry of Finance proposed a further hike to 19% for January 2026 but it was opposed by government leaders and did not take effect; PwC, vatcalc, and tradingeconomics list 18% as the active 2026 rate.

claims.claim: Effective 1 January 2026, Income Tax Amendment 288 widened the 20% bracket to 228,000 NIS annually and the 31% bracket to 301,200 NIS annually; the 35% floor moved to 301,201.
claims.evidence: Globes (2026-03) and CWS Israel describe Amendment 288 widening the 20% band to NIS 19,000/month (228,000/year) and the 31% band to NIS 25,100/month (301,200/year). Top 47% + 3% surtax above 721,560 was not changed.

claims.claim: Credit point value (nekudat zikui) for 2026 is 2,904 NIS/year (242 NIS/month), unchanged from 2025.
claims.evidence: CWS Israel 2026 guide states '₪242/month (₪2,904/year). Value increases if tax bracket freeze is lifted.' The credit point value is frozen alongside other thresholds for 2025-2027.

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi employee rates are 0.40% up to 7,122 NIS/month and 7.00% from 7,123 up to the 49,030 NIS/month ceiling for 2026.
claims.evidence: VOLEH and CWS Israel 2026 references confirm the ~7% rate up to the 49,030 NIS/month ceiling; Bituach Leumi (btl.gov.il) publishes the threshold tables annually with these same figures.

claims.claim: Health tax (mas briut) for 2026: 3.10% up to 7,122 NIS/month, 5.00% above (employee side).
claims.evidence: CWS Israel 2026 confirms health tax was kept at 2025 levels alongside Bituach Leumi for 2026.

claims.claim: From 1 April 2026 the Israeli statutory minimum wage is 6,443.85 NIS/month and ~35.40 NIS/hour at the 182-hour basis (34.64 NIS/hour at the 186-hour basis).
claims.evidence: Histadrut announced a 3.3% rise from 6,247.67 to 6,443.85 NIS/month effective 1 April 2026. Bituach Leumi confirms the same figures; wageindicator.org reports both hourly bases.

claims.claim: For an 18% VAT, gross = net*1.18, net = gross/1.18, VAT-on-gross = gross - gross/1.18 = gross*0.18/1.18.
claims.evidence: Standard inclusive/exclusive VAT identities; algebraically: if gross = net*(1+r), then net = gross/(1+r) and vat = gross - net = gross*r/(1+r). At r=0.18, gross*0.18/1.18 yields the same VAT amount as gross - gross/1.18.

claims.claim: Employees and self-employed taxpayers who contribute to a recognized pension fund get a 35% tax credit on qualifying pension contributions under Section 45a of the Income Tax Ordinance, subject to annual ceilings.
claims.evidence: Carried forward from skill v1.2.0; ITA Section 45a continues to grant 35% credit on the employee-side pension deposit; no change between 2025 and 2026.

claims.claim: Google Sheets ships a native =AI() formula (powered by Gemini) in 2026 that returns text from a natural-language prompt in a cell.
claims.evidence: Google Workspace Updates blog announced general availability of Gemini in Sheets including =AI() formula in 2026. DataCamp and ModelMonkey published tutorials in 2026 documenting the =AI() function as a built-in formula.

claims.claim: LET and LAMBDA are available in Excel for Microsoft 365, Excel for the Web, and Excel 2021/2024 (and remain available in 2026 builds).
claims.evidence: Microsoft Support documents LET as available in Excel 2021 or later and Microsoft 365, and LAMBDA in Microsoft 365 / Excel 2024 / Excel for the web. No deprecation in 2026.

claims.claim: Excel's sort on Hebrew text reliably orders by the first letter but falls back to Unicode codepoint order for subsequent letters in many builds; sorted Hebrew name lists routinely contain ordering errors.
claims.evidence: Documented in multiple user threads (MrExcel, Chandoo, ExcelForum) across Excel 2013-2020. Microsoft's own 2003 documentation flagged the shift from Hebrew code-page sort to Unicode collation, which is the root cause: Unicode codepoint order is not Hebrew alphabetical order beyond the first letter in all words.

claims.claim: Hebcal has no native spreadsheet function; the supported paths are CSV export from hebcal.com or a REST call (https://www.hebcal.com/hebcal) from Apps Script.
claims.evidence: Hebcal's developer-APIs page documents the REST endpoint and CSV/iCal/JSON output formats. The hebcal-rest-api GitHub repo confirms CSV is a first-class export. Apps Script's UrlFetchApp is the standard way to call REST from Google Sheets.

claims.claim: Excel accepts both `#,##0.00 "₪"` and `#,##0.00 [$₪-he-IL]` as Israeli NIS currency formats; the locale-tagged variant survives re-saves by non-Israeli Excel installations more reliably.
claims.evidence: Office Watch (2025) discusses how Excel labels currency entries by language tag rather than country, and shows the [$<symbol>-<locale>] convention used for locale-tagged custom formats. The 40D hex code is the legacy LCID for he-IL but modern Excel accepts the BCP-47 form `he-IL` directly inside the [$...] block.

claims.claim: Merged cells inside an Excel sheet with rightToLeft=true sometimes render in the wrong direction when opened by an Excel installation with a non-Israeli system locale.
claims.evidence: Recurring issue reported on Chandoo and MrExcel threads about RTL + merged-cells interaction. Microsoft Learn documents the Worksheet.DisplayRightToLeft VBA property but does not guarantee merged-span alignment across locales.

claims.claim: Google Sheets `=GOOGLEFINANCE("CURRENCY:USDILS")` returns the current USD to ILS exchange rate.
claims.evidence: Carried forward from skill v1.2.0; Google's official Sheets function documentation lists CURRENCY:<from><to> as a valid GOOGLEFINANCE attribute and USDILS continues to work in 2026.

claims.claim: openpyxl exposes `ws.sheet_view.rightToLeft = True` to mark a worksheet as RTL.
claims.evidence: openpyxl documentation defines SheetView.rightToLeft as a writable bool that maps to the OOXML <sheetView rightToLeft="1"/> attribute, which Excel honors on open. Verified by inspecting the skill's bundled script which has used this property since v1.0.
